programme.title: Secret Nazi Bases
programme.desc: After the Allied landing in Normandy, France falls city after city. An island fortress off the coast becomes the stage for an act of Nazi defiance. Its commanders vow to hold it to the last man.

programme.title: Bobby Robson: More Than a Manager
programme.desc: Documentary exploring the career of the much-loved former England, Barcelona and Newcastle United manager. programme.title: My Icon: Thierry Henry
programme.desc: The former Arsenal and France forward speaks about the individuals that have been an inspiration to him over the course of his career.

programme.title: Boyzone: No Matter What
programme.desc: Documentary about the boy band, featuring rare and exclusive interviews with the members, who talk candidly about how they grappled with the true cost of global fame.

programme.title: Boyzone: No Matter What
programme.desc: The documentary charts the band's growing success with their first number one single. But as their popularity soars, so does the tabloid mania.

programme.title: Boyzone: No Matter What
programme.desc: The documentary charts the band's split which came to an end when Mikey proposed a reunion. The comeback proved a huge hit - but then tragedy struck and the tabloids descended.

programme.title: One to One: John & Yoko
programme.desc: Documentary examining the 18 months that John Lennon and Yoko Ono spent living in Greenwich Village in New York City in the early 1970s, featuring restored footage of Lennon's only full-length, post-Beatles concert.

programme.desc: Documentary shedding new light on the turbulent private lives of Stan Laurel and Oliver Hardy and the genuine friendship between them.

programme.desc: A profile of the English star of stage and screen, who appeared as the villainous Guy of Gisborne in The Adventures of Robin Hood and played Sherlock Holmes in 14 films.

programme.title: Discovering: Jon Voight
programme.desc: A look at the life and career of the actor, who starred in Midnight Cowboy, Deliverance and Runaway Train and won an Academy Award for his role in 1978 film Coming Home.

programme.desc: The life and work of Sidney Lumet, who was Oscar-nominated for his direction of 12 Angry Men, Dog Day Afternoon, Network and The Verdict.

programme.title: My Icon: Heather Watson
programme.desc: British tennis star Heather Watson discusses the individuals who have inspired her over the course of her life.

programme.desc: Set over the course of one night in New York, this documentary invites John McEnroe to reflect on his life and career. programme.title: Brazilians in the Premier League

programme.title: David Attenborough's Galapagos
programme.desc: The broadcaster concludes his exploration of the archipelago with a look at how, despite their proximity, no two of the 19 islands are the same. He also uncovers evidence that evolution on Galapagos is occurring faster than Charles Darwin could ever have imagined, and encounters Lonesome George, the last remaining giant Pinta Island tortoise, who died just a few weeks after filming in June 2012.
